texto: We had all opted to take City's financial reporting course work, which, in theory, meant we wanted to write about stock prices and corporate takeovers. That, of course, was a joke. No one still in their twenties, and broke, goes into journalism to write about money—a subject in which they still have zero practical experience.
explicacao: Citação de Chris Ayres sobre temas ligados a negocios. “We had all opted to take City's financial reporting course work, which, in theory, meant we wante…” articula negocios, business, journalism em registro de negocios, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e journalism.

frase_original: We had all opted to take City's financial reporting course work, which, in theory, meant we wanted to write about stock prices and corporate takeovers. That, of course, was a joke. No one still in their twenties, and broke, goes into journalism to write about money—a subject in which they still have zero practical experience.

texto: The movies, I thought, have got the soundtrack to war all wrong. War isn't rock 'n' roll. It's got nothing to do with Jimi Hendrix or Richard Wagner. War is nursery rhymes and early Madonna tracks. War is the music from your childhood. Because war, when it's not making you kill or be killed, turns you into an infant. For the past eight days, I'd been living like a five-year-old — a nonexistence of daytime naps, mushy food, and lavatory breaks. My adult life was back in Los Angeles with my dirty dishes and credit card bills.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chris Ayres: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The movies, I thought, have got the soundtrack to war all wrong. War isn't rock 'n' roll. It's go…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, music, soundtrack pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e soundtrack.

frase_original: The movies, I thought, have got the soundtrack to war all wrong. War isn't rock 'n' roll. It's got nothing to do with Jimi Hendrix or Richard Wagner. War is nursery rhymes and early Madonna tracks. War is the music from your childhood. Because war, when it's not making you kill or be killed, turns you into an infant. For the past eight days, I'd been living like a five-year-old — a nonexistence of daytime naps, mushy food, and lavatory breaks. My adult life was back in Los Angeles with my dirty dishes and credit card bills.
